channel.title: Projects on CEDIS
channel.description: CEDIS research, development, and innovation projects, sorted by status and date.

item.title: OctaAnalysis Project
item.description: Ongoing CEDIS project focused on the research, design, validation, and continuous evolution of methods and infrastructures for gamification and learning analytics, with OctaAnalysis as its resulting product.

item.title: Evidentia Review Project
item.description: Ongoing CEDIS project focused on the continuous research, evolution, and validation of methods, workflows, and capabilities for systematic reviews, with Evidentia Review as its resulting product.

item.title: Software for Good
item.description: Software for Good (SpB) is a collective—a community of people and organizations, meanings, actions, solutions, feelings, and energies aligned to generate good through the use of software.

item.title: Super r
item.description: Educational project, part of the ‘Software Requirements’ course, an educational innovation developed by CEDIS at the University of Brasília.

item.title: Canal Ciência Project
item.description: A research project in partnership with IBICT and funded by FINATEC, aimed at enhancing the user experience of the Canal Ciência through the use of gamification.

item.title: Green Software Development Project
item.description: This project aims to highlight the importance of understanding the field of Green Software Development and bringing it into companies.

item.title: Laguna Project
item.description: This project aims to extend the massive processing of a data lake using a cloud architecture, utilizing Amazon Web Services (AWS) as the provider.

item.title: Project DFCris
item.description: A research project in partnership with IBICT and funded by FAP/DF.

item.title: HCI Journey Project
item.description: Teaching project, “Human-Computer Interaction” course, an educational innovation promoted by CEDIS in the Software Engineering course at the University of Brasília

item.title: Feudal Dawn Project
item.description: Teaching project, “Software Development” course, an educational innovation promoted by CEDIS at the University of Brasília.

item.title: Informational Management Support System for MCTI
item.description: Ongoing project focused on supporting informational management at the Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation.

item.title: Collaborative Digital Transformation Methodology for SPU
item.description: Ongoing project based on service design and user experience for digital transformation at SPU.

item.title: Predictive framework for student engagement and performance in gamified learning environments
item.description: 2025-2026 PIBIC project focused on creating a predictive framework for student engagement and performance in gamified learning environments by combining Octalysis, neuroscientific indicators, learning analytics, and machine learning.

item.title: Serviços PI
item.description: Ongoing project on innovation and efficiency in retrieving intellectual property information at INPI.

item.title: Improving Digital Public Service for Citizens
item.description: Closed project focused on improving digital public services for citizens.

item.title: Nimbus
item.description: Serverless cloud architecture for automatic code grading.

item.title: Artificial intelligence and data analysis for digital transformation
item.description: Ongoing umbrella project focused on applying artificial intelligence, machine learning, natural language processing, and data analysis to digital transformation initiatives.

item.title: Digital innovation and gamification for teaching, outreach, and software engineering
item.description: Ongoing umbrella project focused on research and development of digital strategies, gamification, indicators, and educational products for teaching, science outreach, and software engineering.

item.title: Doarti
item.description: Project for a technological solution to strengthen donation processes in the Federal District.

item.title: Software Factory Laboratory
item.description: Research and development project started in 2018 at the University of Brasília, focused on integrating training, applied research, and software development.

item.title: A3M: Educational innovation with active methodologies
item.description: Ongoing project associated with the Learning for the Third Millennium Program, focused on educational innovation, active methodologies, and learning indicators at the University of Brasília.
